name: shopping_journey

observed_strengths.observation: 5.0/5 friction score — full journey, 4 clicks to checkout, no blockers; first PDP (e-gift code) failed add-to-cart, crawler correctly fell back to second PDP (Atlas Sweatpant) and succeeded

12 payment methods — the widest coverage of all 5 stores tested: Visa, Mastercard, Amex, PayPal, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Shop Pay, Klarna, Stripe, Discover, Diners Club, JCB; Klarna for BNPL, Google Pay for Android users, Stripe as fallback
Trust badges confirmed at checkout — payment security signals present at the final pre-payment step

missing_or_weak.observation: cart_upsell_present: FALSE — no product recommendations appeared in cart after adding the Atlas Sweatpant; 'complete the look' or 'frequently bought with' is absent, missing the highest-value AOV lift opportunity at the pre-checkout step

urgency_elements_present: FALSE — despite the homepage advertising 'Free Shipping on All Orders. Ends Friday at Midnight.', no urgency signal (countdown, threshold bar, or expiring offer) appears in the cart itself where it would have maximum effect on fence-sitters

No mission reinforcement in cart — the '10 trees per item' promise does not appear in the cart heading structure; at the highest-anxiety checkout step, the unique value proposition (planting trees) that differentiates tentree from commodity apparel is absent

purchase_path_notes: Best payment coverage of all 5 stores (12 methods). Cart mechanics (friction, trust) are solid. The gaps are all AOV and messaging: no upsell, no urgency, no mission reinforcement. The '10 trees' story that closes the brand loop is present on PDPs but absent from the cart.
